subject: Change metrics and transactions layout in logback.xml
commit_message:
Change metrics and transactions layout in logback.xml

Update logging code to compliant with logging standard

Issue-ID: POLICY-2578
Change-Id: I98decce81eae95c006d6485bf3440ac9119b2bf5
Signed-off-by: Joseph Chou <jc2555@att.com>

entries.message:

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/1209/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/1209/ )

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/1209

Patch Set 1:

The purpose of this code update is to make feature-controlloop-trans output compliant with logging standard, only audit and metrics log apply.

Patch Set 15: -Code-Review

> Patch Set 15:
> 
> (1 comment)

Isn't it better to just leave the logback files out altogether?  Can include them in the CSITs and oom instead, right, where they need not include all of these extra fields?  Companies can add their own for deployment via kubernetes, right?

Patch Set 16: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/1231/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/1231

Patch Set 16:

> Patch Set 15: -Code-Review
> 
> > Patch Set 15:
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> 
> Isn't it better to just leave the logback files out altogether?  Can include them in the CSITs and oom instead, right, where they need not include all of these extra fields?  Companies can add their own for deployment via kubernetes, right?

My comments are along making sure the default logback.xml is consistent with the base code in the image.

I think in the OOM if the defaults are OK, I don't see a major reason to create a duplicate of it to just put it outside.   I think it is documented how to override it (I'll make sure it is if not), which is pretty straighforward.   For CSITs, I can see the argument more clear to put it in the mounted directory as it is more used for development, and don't have the size limitations of the charts.
